benefit: Lowest-risk way to convert planning evidence into an explicit behavior contract without changing runtime behavior.
risk: Can clarify desired behavior but cannot prove implementation behavior.
tests_required: Documentation consistency checks; forbidden-claim scan; no runtime diff check
recommendation: Include as part of the next contract-first lane.

benefit: Creates an executable safety net for brevity, claim boundaries, evidence boundaries, and artifact stop conditions before behavior changes.
risk: Fixture design could overfit Batch B unless cases are generalized and official scored artifacts are protected.
files_likely_touched: tests/golden or other offline test fixtures; tests focused on Alpha behavior contracts
tests_required: Short-answer-first golden tests; no invented scaffolding tests; claim-boundary safe wording tests; evidence-boundary tests; artifact stop-condition tests; no live-provider-call check
recommendation: Make this the first implementation step.

benefit: Narrow possible behavior improvement for answer structure, concise caveats, and safe wording once tests exist.
risk: Can change user-visible output and may regress lift or brevity if not constrained by tests.
files_likely_touched: Prompt/protocol text only if explicitly approved; possibly alpha_solver_portable.py only if the prompt text lives there and explicit approval is given
tests_required: All offline golden tests plus regression checks that no provider/model/routing behavior changed
recommendation: Consider only after tests and behavior-contract wording are reviewed.

benefit: Could enforce short-answer-first shape and prevent unnecessary sections in deterministic assembly seams.
risk: More invasive runtime behavior change; may affect broad answer formatting and operator workflows.
files_likely_touched: Answer assembly modules; possibly alpha_solver_portable.py if explicitly approved
tests_required: Golden tests across response modes; regression tests for evidence and safety caveats; no live-provider-call check
recommendation: Do not start in the immediate next lane.

benefit: Could centralize limited-evidence and non-claim wording to reduce overclaim risk.
risk: Helper misuse could create boilerplate, hide nuance, or become a runtime wording dependency before tests are mature.
files_likely_touched: Safe wording helper module or prompt text only if explicitly approved; docs/specs
tests_required: Claim-boundary safe wording tests; no validation/superiority claims; evidence-boundary tests
recommendation: Draft wording in docs first; defer runtime helper implementation.

benefit: Could choose compact answer shapes for reviewer notes, go/no-go decisions, and constrained summaries.
risk: May become hidden routing or policy selection; higher risk than wording/tests.
files_likely_touched: Response mode selection logic if explicitly approved; docs/specs; tests
tests_required: Mode selection golden tests; no invented scaffolding tests; routing non-change verification
recommendation: Do not implement yet; keep as a future proposal.

benefit: Could preserve Alpha lift on high-headroom prompts while avoiding over-answering on low-headroom prompts.
risk: Highest risk because it can overlap provider orchestration, routing, model selection, and live-call behavior.
files_likely_touched: Selective engagement planning docs only unless a later lane explicitly approves code
tests_required: Gate fixture tests; false-positive/false-negative tests; routing/provider non-change checks; no live provider calls
recommendation: Do not implement in the next lane.
